PhysioPage.title: Scientific rigour, human attention.
PhysioPage.lead: I’m a physiotherapist, trained in Belgium, with a passion for movement, health and performance. My path has taken me through varied settings: from professional sport to general private practice, and specialised fields such as senology and geriatrics.
PhysioPage.parcoursP1: These experiences let me discover different approaches to physiotherapy and develop a rich, nuanced view of care. I learned there is no single way to treat - but a multitude of paths, to adapt to each patient.
PhysioPage.parcoursP2: My practice draws on scientifically validated techniques while giving listening an essential place. Every person is unique, with their own story, goals and needs. My role is to understand that uniqueness and offer tailored support.
PhysioPage.parcoursP3: Driven by a sincere desire to help, I commit to guiding my patients towards their best version - in recovery, prevention or performance.
PhysioPage.trailP1: A trail runner myself, I know the demands of effort and recovery from the inside - an understanding of sport that informs every session.
PhysioPage.formationsIntro: My training has been guided by a constant drive to acquire skills recognised by leading bodies, so as to offer care grounded in best practice.
formations: After earning my physiotherapy degree in Brussels, I obtained the Swiss equivalence of my diploma from the Swiss Red Cross, allowing me to practise in Switzerland.
I then completed training in Manual Lymphatic Drainage – Leduc Method, a reference method taught by the official trainers of the Leduc School, recognised internationally.

Wanting to integrate modern, scientifically validated techniques, I trained in Dry Needling at the David G. Simons Academy, an internationally renowned body specialising in the treatment of myofascial pain.
In parallel, I undertook a long course in Sports Manual Therapy in Belgium, deepening my knowledge of biomechanics, manual therapy and the care of athletes, from amateur to elite level.

Convinced that continuing education is essential to quality care, I regularly pursue further training. In particular, I attend training days at the Centre Suisse LPG to stay up to date on the latest LPG technologies, their treatment protocols and the recommendations for endermologie, scar and drainage care.

Today, I keep building my skills across my three areas of expertise: general physio, sports physio and dermato-functional physio, by attending various seminars and exchanging with other healthcare professionals.

SoinsPage.title: Precise treatment, guided by science.
SoinsPage.lead: Medical (with or without a prescription), sport and aesthetic - individualised and recognised by Swiss basic insurance.
SoinsPage.medicalP: On prescription or direct access, precise care for musculoskeletal conditions: orthopaedics, traumatology, functional rehabilitation and acute or chronic pain.
SoinsPage.sportP: A first 30-minute session to assess your needs, then personalised follow-up. For athletes and amateurs alike: goal planning with medical follow-up, coaching and recovery.
SoinsPage.estheticP: Lymphatic drainage, multilayer bandaging, scar work and LPG® sessions - with or without a prescription, for visible, lasting results.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: This policy describes how data is processed on this site, in line with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nLPD).
privacy.dataText: No health data is collected through this site. The contact details you share (email, phone) are used only to answer your request.
privacy.cookiesText: With your consent, Google Analytics 4 measures site audience (Consent Mode v2, anonymised IP). You can decline or withdraw your consent at any time via the cookie banner.
privacy.mapsText: The practice map (Google Maps) is only loaded after you accept cookies.
privacy.rightsText: You have the right to access, correct and delete your data. For any request, contact Thomas Derême.
privacy.note: This document should be reviewed by a professional before going live.

items.a: No. Thomas sees patients with or without a prescription. With a prescription, physiotherapy is recognised by Swiss basic insurance; without one, sessions last 30 or 60 minutes.
Yes - medical physiotherapy on prescription is recognised by Swiss basic insurance (LAMal).
At 12 Rue du Midi, 1003 Lausanne, in Dr Burgener’s Maison-B, 400 m from Lausanne station.

items.q: Do you offer dry needling and lymphatic drainage?
items.a: Yes. Thomas offers dry needling, lymphatic drainage, LPG® endermologie and scar work, among others.
Online via OneDoc, or by phone at +41 76 688 72 81.

SportPage.title: From trail to performance.
SportPage.lead: A trail athlete and physiotherapist, I support athletes - from prevention to performance, up to the highest level.
SportPage.metaDescription: Sports physio in Lausanne: athlete follow-up, prevention and recovery. Thomas Derême, a trail athlete, supports Maximilien Drion.
SportPage.maxText: I support <link>Maximilien Drion</link>, a ski-mountaineering athlete, as a physiotherapist and osteopath in his preparation for international and Olympic competitions.
SportPage.maxText2: A demanding follow-up - injury prevention, recovery and planning - as close as possible to the constraints of elite sport.
SportPage.careText: Assessment, goal planning and personalised follow-up. From a gentle return to competition, I adapt every plan to your practice and your goals.
SportPage.athleteP1: Alongside my work as a physiotherapist, I practise trail running at a high level. This experience lets me understand from the inside the physical, technical and mental demands athletes face - as well as the importance of proper recovery, injury prevention and individualised support.
SportPage.athleteP2: Beyond sport, it reinforces the values that guide my practice every day: listening, rigour, perseverance and care centred on each patient. My goal is to guide everyone, athlete or not, towards lasting recovery - helping them regain confidence in their body, return to their activities in the best conditions and reach their goals safely.
